title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 352.050 - What associations not to incorporate under this chapter — company formed to erect building for use of society, may incorporate
text:
352.050.  What associations not to incorporate under this chapter — company formed to erect building for use of society, may incorporate. — No association, society or company formed for manufacturing, agricultural or business purposes of any kind, or for pecuniary profit in any form, nor any corporation having a capital stock divided into shares, shall be incorporated under this chapter; provided, that any company formed to erect a building for the exclusive use of a society within the purview of this chapter, without pecuniary consideration from such society, may become a body corporate under this chapter notwithstanding it has a capital stock in shares, and may receive subscriptions to such stock, to be paid in real estate, in money, property or services rendered to such company.
(RSMo 1939 § 5444) 
Prior revisions: 1929 § 5003; 1919 § 10271; 1909 § 3439
